name: Montcabrer Summit Loop from Agres
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra de Mariola — Trail run al Montcabrer - Circular desde Agres
description: This difficult 8.9-mile trail run to Montcabrer summit from Agres offers stunning views and a challenging ascent in Serra de Mariola Natural
region_intro: Jogging around Agres offers a diverse range of experiences within a mountainous landscape. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, providing opportunities for invigorating runs with different levels of challenge and elevation. Runners can explore trails that ascend to summits, offering expansive views of the surrounding natural environment. This area is well-suited for those seeking an active outdoor experience amidst a natural backdrop. Best jogging routes around Agres The most popular jogging route is Montcabrer Summit Loop from Agres, a 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain as it loops towards the Montcabrer summit. Another top favourite among local runners is Montcabrer loop from Agres, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail offers a challenging ascent through mountainous terrain, providing a demanding workout. Local runners also love the Alt de la Cava – Cova del Convent loop from Agres, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the local hills, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Agres is defined by mountainous terrain, varied elevations, and summit views. The network offers options for different ability levels, though all listed routes are considered difficult. The routes in Agres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Agres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Agres?
A: There are currently 11 dedicated jogging routes available around Agres on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences within the region's mountainous landscape.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for running trails in Agres?
A: The running trails in Agres are generally considered challenging. All 11 routes listed are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and demanding terrain suitable for experienced runners looking for an invigorating workout.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Agres?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Agres are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872662083/montcabrer-summit-loop-from-agres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit Loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31523166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de la Cava – Cova del Convent loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on running trails near Agres?
A: Jogging in Agres offers breathtaking mountainous scenery with expansive views. The trails often ascend to summits, providing picturesque backdrops of the surrounding natural environment. You'll experience varied terrain characteristic of the Alicante mountain landscape.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Agres?
A: The running routes in Agres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners frequently praise the challenging mountainous terrain, the rewarding summit views, and the invigorating nature of the trails.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural attractions to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region around Agres is rich in history and natural beauty. While on your run, you might encounter sights related to nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3097812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barchell Castle&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocentaina Castle&lt;/a&gt;. There are also interesting caves in the vicinity, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4611508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de les Finestres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Agres?
A: The longest running route currently listed in Agres is a challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt;, spanning approximately 22.9 kilometers (14.2 miles) with significant elevation gain.

Q: Is Agres suitable for trail running events?
A: Yes, the mountainous landscape and varied terrain of Agres are highly suitable for trail running. The area is known to host events like the 'Cabeço Trail of Aigües' (likely referring to Agres), which features distances of 15 km and 21 km, confirming its appeal for dedicated runners.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Agres?
A: Agres enjoys a calm, pleasant, and sunny climate throughout the year, making it conducive to outdoor activities in all seasons. However, for longer or more strenuous runs, spring and autumn might offer the most comfortable temperatures.

Q: Are there any shorter, yet still challenging, running options in Agres?
A: While all routes are rated difficult, you can find shorter challenging options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugi del Montcabrer loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 5 kilometers (3.1 miles) but still features significant elevation, offering a concentrated workout.

Q: Can I find routes that offer summit views in Agres?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Agres lead to or pass by summits, providing spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872662083/montcabrer-summit-loop-from-agres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit Loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, specifically designed to offer these rewarding vistas.

Q: Is the terrain varied enough for different types of running workouts?
A: Yes, the terrain around Agres is highly varied, ranging from trails that ascend steeply into the mountains to paths that traverse local hills. This diversity allows for different types of running workouts, from intense uphill climbs to more undulating trail runs.
tips: In the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. The road ends at the ruins of Mas de Llopis. From this point, a well-marked path takes us in about 45 minutes to the top of Montcabrer.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins at the Santuario de la Mare de Déu d'Agres. You can usually find parking available in the vicinity of the Sanctuary in Agres, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult and requires moderate to high physical effort. It features significant elevation changes, including a demanding initial climb of about 450 meters in roughly 3 km from the Sanctuary. It's best suited for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Montcabrer trail run?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging. You'll encounter steep, winding streets in Agres, well-trodden paths through deep pine forests, and narrower, more exposed sections along the sierra ridge. There are also rocky areas and forest tracks. In colder months, expect potential snow and ice, adding to the difficulty.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers several notable features. You'll reach the summit of Montcabrer Summit, the highest point in the Serra de Mariola Natural Park, offering exceptional 360-degree views. You'll also pass by the historic Cava Gran (or Cava Arquejada), an ancient and well-preserved snow well, and the impressive 16th-century Santuario de la Mare de Déu d'Agres at the start. The route also passes near the Santiago Reig del Moral Refuge.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this trail run?
faqs.answer: Prepare for breathtaking panoramic views! From higher elevations, you'll see expansive vistas of surrounding sierras like Aitana, Serrella, and La Safor, as well as the Beniarrés Reservoir. On clear days, the Mediterranean Sea is visible. The initial ascent provides picturesque views of Agres and its Sanctuary, while the pine forests offer lush scenery and the higher sections dramatic mountain landscapes.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de la Serra de Mariola — Trail run at Montcabrer - Circular from Agres dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in the Parque Natural de la Serra de Mariola, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit to access the trails within the Parque Natural de la Serra de Mariola, and there are no entrance fees. However, as it is a protected natural park, please respect all regulations regarding waste, noise, and environmental preservation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to trail run this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to trail run this route is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter can bring significant challenges, including 20-30 centimeters of snow and slippery ice, especially at higher altitudes. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Senda d'Agres - Cocentaina, Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, El Camí IT5TR4 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and the Senda de les Caves, among others.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of if trail running in winter conditions?
faqs.answer: If you plan to run this trail in winter, be prepared for challenging conditions. Sections can be covered in 20-30 centimeters of snow, which may turn into slippery ice. Proper winter trail running gear, including appropriate footwear with good grip and warm, layered clothing, is essential. Always check the weather forecast and trail conditions beforehand.

faqs.question: Are there any refuges or resting points along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by the Santiago Reig del Moral Refuge (Refugi Montcabrer), which can serve as a potential resting point or shelter during your trail run.
